fields.title: Crime and Punishment
fields.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
fields.description: Crime and Punishment (1866) by Fyodor Dostoevsky is a psychological masterpiece focusing on Rodion Raskolnikov, an impoverished former student in St. Petersburg who murders a pawnbroker to test a theory that extraordinary people can bypass moral law. The novel explores his intense guilt, paranoia, and eventual redemption through suffering, guided by the selfless Sonia.

fields.title: The Divine Comedy
fields.author: Dante Alighieri
fields.description: Dante AlighieriÆs The Divine Comedy is one of the greatest works of world literature. It describes the authorÆs journey through Hell, Purgatory, and Paradise, guided first by Virgil and then by Beatrice. The poem explores themes of morality, divine justice, redemption, and the soulÆs path toward God.

fields.title: Don Quixote
fields.author: Miguel de Cervantes
fields.description: Don Quixote is a foundational work of Western literature by Miguel de Cervantes. It follows the story of Alonso Quixano, a man who becomes convinced he is a knight-errant and embarks on a series of misguided adventures across Spain with his companion Sancho Panza. The novel satirizes chivalric romances while exploring themes of illusion, reality, and human idealism.

fields.title: Pride and Prejudice
fields.author: Jane Austen
fields.description: Romantic novel about manners and marriage.

fields.title: Wuthering Heights
fields.author: Emily Brontδ
fields.description: A dark tale of love and revenge.

fields.title: The Great Gatsby
fields.author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
fields.description: American dream and tragedy.

fields.title: Jane Eyre
fields.author: Charlotte Brontδ
fields.description: Orphan girlÆs journey in life and love.

fields.title: The Hobbit
fields.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
fields.description: A hobbit's unexpected journey.

fields.title: The Fellowship of the Ring
fields.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
fields.description: The beginning of the Ring quest.

fields.title: Harry Potter 1
fields.author: J.K. Rowling
fields.description: A boy discovers he is a wizard.

fields.title: A Game of Thrones
fields.author: George R.R. Martin
fields.description: Political war for the throne.

fields.title: Dune
fields.author: Frank Herbert
fields.description: Desert planet and political struggle.

fields.author: George Orwell
fields.description: Surveillance state and control.

fields.title: Brave New World
fields.author: Aldous Huxley
fields.description: Engineered society of control.

fields.title: Foundation
fields.author: Isaac Asimov
fields.description: Fall of a galactic empire.

fields.title: Neuromancer
fields.author: William Gibson
fields.description: Hacker world and AI.

fields.title: Dracula
fields.author: Bram Stoker
fields.description: Classic vampire story.

fields.title: Frankenstein
fields.author: Mary Shelley
fields.description: Creation and tragedy.

fields.title: The Shining
fields.author: Stephen King
fields.description: The Shining is a 1977 horror novel by American author Stephen King. It is King's third published novel and first hardcover bestseller; its success firmly established King as a preeminent author in the horror genre. The setting and characters are influenced by King's personal experiences, including both his visit to The Stanley Hotel in 1974 and his struggle with alcoholism. The novel was adapted into a 1980 film and a 1997 miniseries. The book was followed by a sequel, Doctor Sleep, published in 2013, which in turn was adapted into a 2019 film.

fields.title: Meditations
fields.author: Marcus Aurelius
fields.description: Meditations by Marcus Aurelius is a cornerstone of Stoic philosophy, written as personal journal entries by the Roman Emperor (161û180 AD) while on campaign. It offers practical guidance on cultivating mental toughness, virtue, self-control, and resilience against life's hardships, advising readers to accept what is beyond their control.

fields.title: The Republic
fields.author: Plato
fields.description: The Republic, written by Plato around 380 BC, is a foundational Socratic dialogue exploring justice, the nature of a just city-state, and the ideal human soul. Using dialogues between Socrates and others, it examines whether a just person is happier than an unjust one, defining virtue, political structure, and the nature of reality.

fields.title: The Alchemist
fields.author: Paulo Coelho
fields.description: The Alchemist by Paulo Coelho is a celebrated philosophical novel and modern classic that follows Santiago, an Andalusian shepherd boy, on his journey to find worldly treasure. The story is an allegorical quest to discover one's "Personal Legend"ùor true purpose in lifeùby listening to one's heart and reading the omens of the universe.

fields.title: The Road
fields.author: Cormac McCarthy
fields.description: The Road by Cormac McCarthy is a 2006 Pulitzer Prize-winning post-apocalyptic novel detailing the harrowing journey of a father and his young son across a ravaged, lifeless American landscape. They travel south, surviving extreme hunger and violent gangs, searching for warmth and safety while maintaining their humanity through deep love.

fields.title: The Book Thief
fields.author: Markus Zusak
fields.description: The Book Thief by Markus Zusak is a historical novel set in Nazi Germany, narrated by Death, following young Liesel Meminger as she finds comfort in stealing books and learning to read amid the horrors of World War II. Living with foster parents, Liesel shares these stories with her neighbors during bombings and a hidden Jewish man, Max.

fields.title: Sherlock Holmes
fields.author: Arthur Conan Doyle
fields.description: The Sherlock Holmes books are classic detective fiction by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle, featuring a brilliant, eccentric private consulting detective in late 19th-century London. Known for his profound observational skills,, mastery of deductive reasoning, and forensics, Holmes works with his loyal friend Dr. John Watson to solve baffling cases and help.

fields.title: Gone Girl
fields.author: Gillian Flynn
fields.description: Gone Girl by Gillian Flynn is a2012 #1 New York Times bestselling psychological thriller about a marriage gone wrong, focusing on the disappearance of Amy Dunne on her fifth wedding anniversary. The story, set in Missouri, centers on her husband, Nick, who becomes the primary suspect as the investigation reveals dark secrets, deception, and media manipulation in their relationship.

fields.title: Anna Karenina
fields.author: Leo Tolstoy
fields.description: Leo Tolstoy's Anna Karenina (1878) is a masterpiece of realist literature exploring passion, jealousy, and social hypocrisy in 19th-century Russia. It contrasts the tragic, scandalous affair of the married Anna Karenina with Count Vronsky against the idyllic, moral courtship of Konstantin Levin and Kitty, exploring themes of familial joy and societal constraints.

fields.title: The Brothers Karamazov
fields.author: Dostoevsky
fields.description: The Brothers Karamazov (1880) by Fyodor Dostoevsky is a monumental philosophical novel masked as a murder mystery, exploring faith, free will, morality, and the Russian soul. It centers on the tumultuous relationships between a vile father, Fyodor, and his three sonsùthe sensualist Dmitri, intellectual Ivan, and spiritual Alyoshaùas they navigate spiritual conflict and psychological drama in 19th-century Russia.

fields.title: Madame Bovary
fields.author: Flaubert
fields.description: Madame Bovary (1857) by Gustave Flaubert is a seminal work of literary realism exploring the tragic life of Emma Bovary, a country doctor's wife who pursues romantic, luxurious ideals to escape her dull, provincial life. Driven by disillusionment and crippling debt, she engages in ruinous extramarital affairs, ultimately leading to her destruction.

fields.title: Fahrenheit 451
fields.author: Ray Bradbury
fields.description: Fahrenheit 451 by Ray Bradbury is a dystopian novel about a future society where books are banned and "firemen" burn any found to suppress independent thought. The story follows fireman Guy Montag, who begins questioning his oppressive, technology-obsessed life after meeting a free-thinking neighbor and stealing books himself.

fields.title: Animal Farm
fields.author: George Orwell
fields.description: Animal Farm is a famous 1945 allegorical novella by George Orwell that satirizes the Stalinist era of the Soviet Union. It tells the story of farm animals who overthrow their human oppressor, only to have their new society betray them and turn into a dictatorship run by pigs, illustrating how power corrupts, even in revolutions.

fields.title: It
fields.author: Stephen King
fields.description: It (1986) is a renowned horror novel by Stephen King that follows seven outcast childrenùthe "Losers' Club"ùas they fight a, shape-shifting, child-killing entity in Derry, Maine. The story alternates between 1958 and 1985, as the now-adult group returns to destroy the evil that feeds on fear.

fields.title: The Silmarillion
fields.author: Tolkien
fields.description: The Silmarillion (1977), edited posthumously by Christopher Tolkien, is a collection of mythopoeic stories detailing the creation and ancient history of Middle-earth, serving as the "Bible" or Silmarillion Wikipedia of J.R.R. Tolkien's legendarium. Set primarily in the First Age, it chronicles the epic, tragic war against the dark lord Morgoth over three stolen jewelsùthe Silmarils.

fields.title: Percy Jackson
fields.author: Rick Riordan
fields.description: Perseus "Percy" Jackson is the demigod son of Poseidon and Sally Jackson, serving as the main protagonist of Rick Riordan's Percy Jackson & the Olympians series. He is described as a loyal, brave, and slightly impulsive leader with disheveled black hair, sea-green eyes, and a lean, muscular build.

fields.title: The Martian
fields.author: Andy Weir
fields.description: The Martian by Andy Weir is a gripping "hard" science fiction survival novel. It follows astronaut Mark Watney, who is accidentally stranded alone on Mars in 2035 and must rely on his wit, engineering skills, and a sardonic sense of humor to survive in the hostile environment after his crew abandons him, thinking he died.

fields.title: Ready Player One
fields.author: Ernest Cline
fields.description: Ready Player One (2011) is a dystopian science fiction novel by Ernest Cline set in 2045, where humanity escapes a bleak reality via a virtual world called the OASIS. Teenage protagonist Wade Watts hunts for a hidden Easter egg left by the game's creator, which promises immense fortune and control of the OASIS.

fields.title: The Odyssey
fields.author: Homer
fields.description: The Odyssey is an ancient Greek epic poem attributed to Homer, chronicling the 10-year, perilous journey of Odysseus, King of Ithaca, trying to return home after the Trojan War. It focuses on themes of adventure, temptation, and loyalty, featuring encounters with mythical creatures, gods, and sorceresses while his wife, Penelope, fends off suitors.

fields.title: The Iliad
fields.author: Homer
fields.description: The Iliad is an ancient Greek epic poem attributed to Homer, set during the final year of the Trojan War. It focuses on the rage of the warrior Achilles, his withdrawal from battle against the Greek leader Agamemnon, and the subsequent devastating consequences for the Greeks. The poem highlights themes of heroism, fate, and divine intervention.

fields.title: Les MisΘrables
fields.author: Victor Hugo
fields.description: Les MisΘrables (1862) by Victor Hugo is an epic historical novel following Jean Valjean, an ex-convict seeking redemption in 19th-century France. Over 19 years after stealing bread, Valjean breaks parole, reforms his life, and is relentlessly pursued by Inspector Javert. The story covers themes of social injustice, love, and sacrifice against the 1832 Paris Uprising.

fields.title: Hamlet
fields.author: Shakespeare
fields.description: Hamlet is a Shakespearean tragedy tracking the Prince of Denmark, who seeks revenge after his father's ghost accuses ClaudiusùHamletÆs uncle and new kingùof murder. Hamlet grapples with hesitation, grief, and feigned madness, resulting in the deaths of the royal court, including himself, his mother Gertrude, and his lover Ophelia.

fields.title: Macbeth
fields.author: Shakespeare
fields.description: Macbeth is a dark, fast-paced Shakespearean tragedy about a Scottish general who, driven by ambition and spurred by supernatural prophecy, murders King Duncan to take the throne. Consumed by guilt and paranoia, Macbeth turns into a tyrannical ruler, launching a bloodbath to secure power, ultimately leading to his destruction.

fields.title: Othello
fields.author: Shakespeare
fields.description: Othello, the Moor of Venice is a major tragedy written by William Shakespeare around 1603, centered on themes of jealousy, manipulation, racism, and betrayal. The play follows Othello, a noble Moorish general in the Venetian army, whose life and marriage are destroyed by his trusted ensign, Iago, who manipulates him into believing his wife, Desdemona, is unfaithful.

fields.title: The Trial
fields.author: Kafka
fields.description: The Trial by Franz Kafka (1925) is a surreal, nightmarish novel detailing the arrest of Josef K., a bank clerk unexpectedly prosecuted by a mysterious, inaccessible authority for an unknown crime. The story explores themes of existential dread, bureaucratic absurdity, and alienation as K. futilely navigates a labyrinthine legal system, culminating in his execution.

fields.title: Invisible Man
fields.author: Ralph Ellison
fields.description: Invisible Man (1952) by Ralph Ellison is a landmark American novel exploring identity and racial invisibility, featuring an unnamed Black narrator navigating systemic racism from the rural South to 1930s Harlem. He experiences betrayal, prejudice, and political manipulation, finally retreating to a basement, recognizing society refuses to see him.

fields.title: Beloved
fields.author: Toni Morrison
fields.description: Beloved by Toni Morrison is a Pulitzer Prize-winning novel (1988) set in post-Civil War Ohio, exploring the traumatic legacy of slavery. It follows Sethe, a formerly enslaved woman haunted by the ghost of her daughter, whom she killed to save from enslavement, and the mysterious arrival of a young woman named Beloved.
